summary: X101_fixed_symmetric_tight is a clean exit-side archetype with no research-integrity concerns; it directly addresses the E126 asymmetric-tail trap and introduces no leakage, credential, or sandbox-boundary risks.

risk_assessments.leakage_notes: novel_dim is only tp_pct=0.5 and sl_pct=0.5 — both are exit thresholds applied forward from T0 inside the simulator. No forward_*, confirm_after_*, post_event_*, or _after_event fields are referenced. Exit kernel is the existing 'fixed' family which is already T0-causal in the loop.
risk_assessments.live_conflict_notes: Exit archetype is channel=NA/side=NA, so it only activates when paired with an entry archetype during the joint search. It can pair with entries that overlap the live BWE_OI_Price_monitor pump long strategy, but this is paper-shadow / backtest only — no live-config write, no order routing. Standard paper-shadow monitoring item, not a blocker.
risk_assessments.remediation: None required. Recommend the loop verifies tp_pct/sl_pct are consumed by the existing 'fixed' exit kernel only (no new code path), and that any pairing with entries currently mirrored to the live OI_Price long strategy is tagged 'live_overlap=true' in results.tsv for paper-shadow journal review — advisory only, do not block scoring.
